zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Joan De Grey於大約1289年出生在英國奧可福爾洛瑟費爾德 格斯時，她的父親Thomas de Grey24歲，母親Maud23歲。她於1316年與Guy de Bretton在英國英格蘭奧可福爾洛瑟費爾德 格斯結婚。他們至少是2個女兒的父母。她去世於1353年4月5日在英國斯塔福爾德雷頓 巴塞特享年65歲。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Grey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish: variant of   Gray  .
In some cases also an American shortened and altered form of Ukrainian Grushko: nickname or topographic name derived from a Russian-influenced transliteration of grushka ‘pear, pear tree’.

variants.name: Gray
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish (especially Eastern Ulster; of Norman origin): habitational name from Graye in Calvados, France, named from the Gallo-Roman personal name Graec(i)us, meaning ‘Greek’ + the locative suffix -acum. This is probably the chief source of the surname in Britain.

English: nickname for someone with gray hair or a gray beard, from Middle English grey (Old English grǣg, grēg) ‘gray’. In Ireland it has been used as a translation of various Gaelic surnames derived from riabhach ‘brindled, gray’, including Mac Giolla Riabhaigh; see McGreevy . In North America, this surname has assimilated names with similar meaning from other languages.
French: habitational name from Gray in Haute-Saône or Le Gray in Seine-Maritime.

variants.name: Greiwe
meanings: German: possibly a nickname from Middle High German greibe ‘sharp, tangy, dour’. Compare Grey .

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Joan De Grey was born about 1289, in Rotherfield Greys, Oxfordshire, England, her father, Thomas de Grey, was 24 and her mother, Maud, was 23. She married Guy de Bretton in 1316, in Rotherfield Greys, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 daughters. She died on 5 April 1353, in Drayton Bassett, Staffordshire, England, at the age of 65.

Elle a épousé Guy de Bretton en 1316, à Rotherfield Greys, Oxfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 5 avril 1353, à Drayton Bassett, Staffordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 65 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Joan De Grey(이)가 대략 1289년 영국 스퍼드실르 로더필드 그리스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas de Grey(은)는 24세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Maud(은)는 23세였다. 그녀는 1316년 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르 로더필드 그리스에서 Guy de Bretton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1353년 4월 5일 영국 스태퍼드실르 드라이턴 배싯에서 65세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Joan De Grey родилась около 1289 г. в местности ротирфиелд грис, оксфордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Thomas de Grey было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Maud, 23. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Guy de Bretton в 1316 г. в местности ротирфиелд грис, оксфордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 дочери. Она умерла 5 апреля 1353 г. в местности дреитон бассетт, стаффордшир, Англия в возрасте 65 лет.
